name: Bagels 4
recipeIngredient: 4 cups All-purpose or bread flour (I prefer high gluten)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the yeast over the warm water, stir until dissolved.
2. In a food processor bowl with dough blade, combine the salt, flour, and 4 tsp sugar. Pulse several times to mix well and aerate.
3. Combine the yeast mixture and milk in a measuring cup. With the processor motor running, feed through the feed tube. Knead until the mixture forms a ball and is no longer sticky (about 60 seconds). Lightly flour an large plastic bag; place the dough in it, remove most of the air, and close with a twist tie. Let rise until doubled (about 45 minutes).
4. Punch down the dough. Let rest for ten minutes.
5. Divide the dough into 12 two-inch balls. Poke each ball with a finger to make a hole the size of a golf ball. Shape each bagel evenly using your fingers. Place on a cookie sheet, cover with oiled plastic wrap, and let rise until puffy (about 30 minutes).
6. Preheat oven to 400°F.
7. Bring 4 quarts water to boil in a wide pot. Add the remaining 2 teaspoons sugar. Poach the bagels, 3 or 4 at a time, for 30 seconds. Turn and poach for another 30 seconds. Remove with a slotted spoon, briefly drain on a towel, and place 1 inch apart on baking sheets.
8. Brush each bagel with a little egg glaze. Sprinkle optional coarse salt or seeds over the bagels if desired. Bake until golden brown (about 15 minutes). Cool on racks.
